content.full_text:
DRUG USE
IS LIFE ABUSE

MEMBERS OF THE CITY COUNCIL
RONALD J. CURTIS
LAWRENCE F. BUCHHEIM
KENNETH E. FRIECH
GARY L. HAUSDORFER
PHILLIP R. SCHWARTZE

CITY MANAGER
STEPHEN B JULIAN

March 1, 1991

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Request for Advice

Dear Hallabrin:

This letter is submitted to request an informal written opinion as to whether I, acting as City Attorney, would have a possible conflict of interest in giving legal advice to the City Planning Commission with respect to a proposed subdivision coming before the City's Planning Commission on March 12, 1991 for a public hearing.

The proposed subdivision is to be developed by GlenFed Development, Inc. on a project generally known as the GlenFed H Area within the City of San Juan Capistrano.

The facts are that this developer is proposing a 60-lot subdivision within a development called the GlenFed H Area as shown on the attached Exhibit A. I, as City Attorney, am called upon to give legal advice regarding legal questions that may arise with respect to the processing of the subdivision tentative map at the time that the subdivision comes before the Planning Commission.

I own a single family home within a subdivision known as the Los Corrales Tract which is located adjacent to the project site. My home is .6 miles (that is, 3,168 feet) from the project boundary line nearest to my home. The approximate value of my home is $350,000 (see Exhibit A).

A public hearing has been set for March 12, 1991 wherein the Planning Commission will hold its first public hearing on the proposed Tentative Subdivision Map for the GlenFed H Area project. Concerned citizens have already expressed concern over several issues, including traffic circulation emanating from the project site and traveling down Camino La Ronda to La Novia (see Exhibit A).

The operative FPPC regulation appears to be Section 18702.3(b) which provides:

32400 PASEO ADELANTO, SAN JUAN CAPISTRANO, CALIFORNIA 92675 • (714) 493-1171

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
March 1, 1991
Page Two


"(b)    The reasonably foreseeable effect of a decision is not
considered material as to real property in which an
official has a direct, indirect or beneficial interest (not
including a leasehold interest), if the real property in
which the official has an interest is located entirely
beyond a 2,500 foot radius of the boundaries (or the
proposed boundaries) of the property which is the subject
of the decision unless

(1)    There are specific circumstances regarding the
decision, its effect, and the nature of the real
property in which the official has an interest,
which make it reasonably foreseeable that the fair
market value or the rental value of the real
property in which the official has an interest will
be affected by the amounts set forth in
subdivisions (a) (3) (A) or (a) (3) (B); and

(2)    Either of the following apply:

(A)    The effect will not be substantially the same
as the effect upon at least 25 percent of all
the properties which are within a 2,500 foot
radius of the boundaries of the real property
in which the official has an interest; or

(B)    There are not at least 10 properties under
separate ownership within a 2,500 foot
radius of the property in which the official
has an interest."

In light of the above facts, I request an informal written opinion as to whether Section
18702.3(b) would bar me, as legal counsel to the Planning Commission, from participating
on the proposed subdivision map at the March 12 Planning Commission hearing.

Your cooperation is most appreciated.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
JOHN R. SHAW
City Attorney

JRS/ef

Figure 9
Location of Other Developments

Legend

B Health Park
C San Juan Corporate Plaza
D Area A/Plaro del Obispo
E Historic Town Center
F Rancho San Juan
G Franciscan Plaza
H Glendale Federal Properties

① Paseo La Branca
② Via Pantero
③ Via Herrera
④ Calle San Remo
⑤ Via Montura
⑥ Paseo Corrales
⑦ Paseo Estudio

Kunzman Associates

EXHIBIT A
